And so you have that but what's really missing from this, right? Let's say everything remained the same. We didn't mess with any of the copy or anything. How could we have maximized this? Well, the obvious thing is number one, we could have put a freaking order bump on here, you know, like, hey, they're already spending $297 here. What if we said, hey, do you want to add something else is really cool to your order for, I don't know, you know, 37 bucks, 47 bucks, like most likely around 20% or so, the folks who bought would have taken me up on that. That's just free money, right? Very rarely will that actually hurt your conversion rate. Another place where we went wrong is let's go back over here to our stats is I have nothing in place to follow up with the folks who started order. Notice I got 55 people started to order here, 37 people started to order here. I have no follow up sequence for these guys, right? So like there's no card abandonment. There should have been card abandonment. It could have got me, you know, where there's what like, let's say 20, actually, yeah, exactly 20 people that abandoned the cart and didn't buy. Dude, that's like, I might have been able to get one or two sales out of that, which is still 400 bucks from for writing emails, you know, probably should have had some emails going to people who didn't buy. They started to they didn't. Who knows why, but what else is missing? Yeah, one click up sells like this is totally moronic, not to have one click up sells. They they're literally just spending $200. Like why wouldn't I offer them something else? I got in a hurry and I didn't think about it until after the fact. And I was like, well, shoot, I don't want to mess with the thing, you know, but surely some one click up sells would be more profitable. In fact, I think it's reasonable to assume that I would have that it would have literally been physically impossible to make less money if I had one click up sells, because even if nobody took them, all right, it wouldn't have had any effect on the upfront conversion because someone would never even see the one click up sell until they've made the purchase anyway. So I missed out on like 60 something 70 72 71. So one of these is a test order. I missed out on 72 opportunities to sell something on a one click up sell, even if it was something cheap. All right, so let's say that it was like 97 bucks, you know, so they're buying something for 300 and they add another thing for $97. Who knows what it could be. And I only sold like 10% of them. Well, that's another $700, you know, and that like 700 bucks matters, right. But regardless, we made 21 grand from a clearly imperfectly executed campaign. My point in showing you all of this is like, look, we're generating ads or we're using ads, we're getting leads, we're getting customers, we're getting momentum. Like we want to really squeeze every last drop we can out of what we're using, even if we do it imperfectly, it's going to beat not doing anything. Right. So deploy this stuff, like really go out and make offers, the more offers you make, the more money you make, you know, and what's really crazy is I haven't emailed my list in a while and I'm looking at this, you know, I'm talking to my wife, I'm like, my gosh, we made 20 grand this week, just messing around with this, you know, I mean, the offer itself was good, but the webpage was terrible. And I'm thinking if I did that every week, it'd be an extra $80,000 a month, like every 80 grand helps, you know, so let's all get to work and take note of how easy it is to do this and also take note of how easily we could have been foiled if we stopped, if we gave up and if we let ourselves fall victim to oh, you know, so I'm sure my support team got nasty emails, you dummy, you can't even sell anything, your video doesn't work or whatever. If we had paid attention to that and not pressed forward, it would be like writing a check to them for $21,000. Don't write checks to fear. Right. Let other people write checks to you. Hope you like this series. Now get to work and sell some stuff.
